body: ## Purpose - previously, no cpu benchmarks were run, and the artifacts produced in buildkite were not uploaded by the pipeline - add NVTX annotations ## To-do Maybe reduce the number of NVTX annotations ## Content - NVTX added as a dependency. Annotations added to cache updates, implicit tendencies, explicit tendencies, and boundary flux updates. Uses default naming and domains - adds CPU runs to benchmark pipeline. - reduce code duplication in the benchmarks. move shared functions to a benchmarking utils file - add a soil only benchmark - use the LandSimulation struct in benchmarks, update them to work with new LandParams - Save integrated gpu profiling results to csv. Fix issue where profiler would stall and builkdite job would run until its time limit. - Change nsys profiling to not profile compilation/setup. Enables additional NVTX callbacks for allocation, freeing memory, and inference. - Make buildkite job only use 2 gpus at once - Add a buildkite pipeline that is conditionally triggered based on changes. It only runs gpu benchmarks, and only for a single model per PR ---- - [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.

labels.description: Add this label to run benchmarks on clima
labels.name: Run benchmarks

title: Refactor Benchmarks, add NVTX

body:
Bumps [julia-actions/setup-julia](https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia) from 2 to 3.
Release notes

Sourced from julia-actions/setup-julia's releases.

v3.0.0

:warning: Breaking Changes, and Migration Guide for v2 ⟶ v3

  1. v3 requires Node 24 (compared to v2, which required Node 20).
  2. The behavior of version: min has changed between v2 and v3.
    • In v2, version: min would resolve to the minimum major/minor/patch, e.g. 1.10.0.
    • In v3, version: min resolves to the minimum major/minor but the latest patch, e.g. 1.10.11.
    • If you specifically want the minimum major/minor/patch under v3, you should specify version: min-patch. Note: v3 also adds the alias version: min-minor, which is equivalent to version: min.
  3. v3 will throw an error if x86_64 Julia binaries are requested on Apple Silicon macOS. (Under v2, this was a warning.) Under v3, if you specifically want to run x86_64 Julia binaries on Apple Silicon (under Rosetta 2), you must now opt-in by specifying the force-arch: true input.

Other Changes

We updated some dependencies, and we improved and updated the CI tooling on this repo.

Full Changelog

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v2.7.0...v3.0.0

v2.7.0

body: ## Purpose Current main after 20 years spin up: Current main with energy runoff included: The runoff scheme included here: Bottom soil moisture looks reasonable everywhere after 20 years (nanmask = ocean mask) julia> extrema(swc_data["swc"][end, :, :, 1][nanmask]) (0.08502579603124887, 0.6976980101713615) We still have some high levels closer to the surface - up to 1.25, but this is significantly better than the values of 7 we were getting at all levels before. I think this indicates we have some further work to do on surface runoff. ## To-do ## Content ---- - [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Energy of subsurface runoff + alleviate issue with excess water
